authoritative_sources.title: Business Guide to the FTC's Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for remote-order handling. This guide is authoritative for delay, cancellation, prompt refund, and order-record expectations when grocery inventory shortages affect online, phone, pickup, or delivery orders.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customer order, delay, cancellation, and refund-boundary review
used_for: prompt-refund escalation posture without approving refunds in-lane
customer order record expectations when inventory cannot satisfy demand

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Current Edition
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public model used by many U.S. jurisdictions for legal metrology, price verification, unit pricing, method of sale, and package labeling. It is authoritative for grocery inventory cases where weighed items, substitutions, unit of measure, or promotions create pricing-sensitive customer impact.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: unit-price, method-of-sale, weighed-item, and price-sensitive substitution review
used_for: evidence expectations for random-weight, PLU, unit, package, and POS-linked inventory records
customer-facing price or promotion conflict handling

authoritative_sources.title: Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the public model food-safety reference used by many state, local, tribal, and territorial retail food regulators. It is authoritative for approved-source, receiving, temperature, contamination, date marking, shellstock, and hold boundaries that grocery inventory work must not override.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: receiving, hold, temperature, approved-source, and saleability stop-condition review
used_for: perishable, refrigerated, frozen, prepared-food, and shellstock inventory boundaries
no release of safety-questioned inventory from this lane

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public procurement, supply-chain, inventory, or vendor-management guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability FAQ is the current authoritative public explanation of Food Traceability List receiving records, retail food establishment responsibilities, record retrieval, and traceability plan expectations that shape grocery receiving and inventory evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability record, receiving evidence, and retrieval-prerequisite review

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulator, statute, agency manual, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: eCFR is the official current regulatory text for the FDA Food Traceability Rule, including critical tracking events, key data elements, record maintenance, and FDA access requirements. It is authoritative when covered-food inventory and receiving records determine safe action.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: regulatory traceability and record-access boundary review
used_for: hold or escalation when covered-food records are missing

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls, Market Withdrawals, & Safety Alerts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes public recall, market withdrawal, and safety alert information for FDA-regulated products, including food and beverages. It is authoritative for stop-sale, hold, and customer-impact handling involving FDA-regulated grocery inventory.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall, withdrawal, stop-sale, and customer-impact review
used_for: customer-harm routing when affected inventory reached orders

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls and Public Health Alerts
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FSIS is the federal public health authority for meat, poultry, and certain egg products. Its recall and public health alert records are authoritative for grocery inventory work involving FSIS-regulated products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: meat, poultry, and egg product recall and hold review
used_for: meat, poultry, and egg product availability stop conditions

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is an authoritative public baseline for recognizing suspicious account, payment, identity, or contact-change activity that should move grocery vendor, refund, customer, or order anomalies to fraud or loss-prevention owners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fraud-sensitive order, vendor, payment, and refund anomaly screening
used_for: suspicious customer or vendor pattern routing

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Standards for Foodservice & Retail Grocery
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US is the recognized standards body for product identifiers, barcodes, locations, shipments, and traceability language used by foodservice and retail grocery trading partners. Its retail grocery guidance is authoritative for vendor-neutral inventory, shipment, and traceability terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: item, location, shipment, and inventory evidence normalization
used_for: GTIN, UPC, barcode, location, shipment, and traceability terminology
vendor-neutral evidence handling across suppliers and retailers
inventory and recall response artifact expectations

workflow_stages.name: Supply or procurement intake
workflow_stages.description: Capture store, department, channel, SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot, vendor, PO, ASN, promotion, order, customer-impact, and authority context before any inventory status or replenishment recommendation is produced.

workflow_stages.description: Compare item master, ledger, shelf, backroom, reserved, inbound, allocated, picked, substituted, on-order, and vendor evidence to decide whether availability can be summarized or must be blocked.

workflow_stages.name: Vendor or inventory action management
workflow_stages.description: Package vendor delays, short ships, allocation cuts, pack-size discrepancies, rejected receiving, PO or invoice mismatches, and substitute-item requests into action notes without approving spend, supplier changes, or product movement.

workflow_stages.name: Exception escalation for shortages, holds, or supplier issues
workflow_stages.description: Escalate required supply shortages, recalls, stop sales, temperature holds, traceability gaps, safety concerns, fraud signals, vendor constraints, and policy conflicts rather than synthesizing unauthorized decisions.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Public procurement, supply-chain, inventory, or vendor-management guidance

workflow_stages.name: High-volume intake or service execution
workflow_stages.description: Triage online pick exceptions, store replenishment queues, promotional set shortages, rain-check exposure, and customer-impact service tickets by record completeness, customer impact, safety posture, and owner.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation
workflow_stages.description: When shortages or substitutions create refund, pricing, promotion, safety, fraud, or service-recovery implications, preserve inventory evidence and route to authorized owners instead of improvising customer remedies.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Close only when authoritative inventory, vendor, customer-impact, and safety evidence supports the output and stricter tenant, local, brand, or supplier rules have been retrieved and recorded.
source_classes: Public procurement, supply-chain, inventory, or vendor-management guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: item master, SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, random-weight, catch-weight, case pack, each, unit of measure, department, category, planogram, shelf location, and product status records
inventory ledger, shelf count, backroom count, cycle count, shrink, spoilage, markdown, reserve, picked, substituted, transfer, allocation, on-order, inbound, and out-of-stock records
purchase orders, replenishment requests, vendor acknowledgements, ASN records, delivery appointments, receiving logs, rejected receiving notes, invoices, credit memos, and vendor-performance notes
POS sales, promotion calendars, ad commitments, online order records, pickup or delivery status, CRM notes, service tickets, refund requests, rain checks, and customer exception notes
recall notices, market withdrawals, stop-sale directives, temperature logs, food-safety holds, traceability lot codes, date codes, shellstock tags, loss-prevention artifacts, and fraud-review notes
availability summaries, replenishment recommendations, vendor-action notes, shortage escalations, traceability evidence-gap summaries, and customer exception escalation notes
systems_of_record: vendor portal or supplier management system

allowed: classify grocery inventory requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, systems of record, and source context
summarize availability, replenishment, vendor, logistics, and shortage status from traceable records
prepare procurement, replenishment, vendor-risk, shortage, or customer-impact packages with constraints and dependencies
route work to adjacent specialists when pricing, refund, fraud, safety, procurement approval, supplier approval, or policy authority shifts
escalate: required supply is unavailable or short and customer service, promotion, online order, or operational commitments are affected
vendor allocation, substitution, approved-supplier status, pack-size change, or procurement decision exceeds documented lane authority
traceability, inventory, receiving, recall, POS, OMS, or vendor evidence is incomplete, stale, or contradictory
customer harm, refund, pricing, promotion, fraud, loss-prevention, safety, recall, or policy exception determines the outcome
public source and tenant, local, franchise, vendor, or brand policy materially conflict
refuse: approve spend, supplier substitutions, supplier onboarding, supplier risk, contract changes, freight expedites, quality release, or inventory movement directly
override pricing, refund, promotion, rain-check, SNAP/EBT, customer recovery, safety, or fraud policies without explicit authority
release recalled, stop-sale, temperature-abused, expired, contaminated, held, or traceability-questioned inventory
adjudicate fraud, theft, coupon abuse, payment anomalies, chargebacks, or loss-prevention findings
close availability, replenishment, or shortage work without authoritative evidence

domain_failure_modes: The lane marks a grocery SKU available because inventory ledger stock exists while shelf, backroom, reserve, recall, or online order evidence blocks saleable availability.
A vendor allocation cut or substitute GTIN is treated as routine replenishment and the lane implies supplier or substitution approval it does not own.
Receiving, lot, traceability, POS, WMS, and OMS records conflict but the lane collapses them into a confident stock answer.
A recalled, stop-sale, temperature-held, expired, contaminated, or traceability-questioned item is routed toward sale or substitution to satisfy service speed.
A stockout that affects an online order is handled as inventory-only and the lane silently approves pricing, refund, customer recovery, or rain-check exceptions.
High-volume pick exceptions are processed without preserving item-level SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot, department, safety, and customer-impact evidence.
Suspicious order spikes, duplicate accounts, coupon patterns, or refund anomalies are treated as ordinary demand instead of routed to fraud or loss-prevention.
Tenant-specific replenishment, substitution, approved-vendor, SNAP/EBT, local health, or authority rules are missing and the lane guesses.
Grocery terminology such as PLU, random weight, catch weight, case pack, shelf cap, ASN, short ship, shrink, spoilage, rain check, and 86/out-of-stock is flattened into generic warehouse language.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: jurisdiction-specific unit pricing, price accuracy, bottle deposit, SNAP/EBT, and retail food rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: No blocking ambiguity for the validated public baseline. The pack requires jurisdiction and tenant policy retrieval before applying local rules.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant-specific substitution, refund, promotion, customer recovery, and approved-supplier authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: No blocking ambiguity for the validated public baseline. The lane refuses or escalates when documented authority is missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FSMA traceability coverage and exemptions for specific retail establishments and food categories
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: No blocking ambiguity for the validated public baseline. The pack treats covered-food traceability evidence as required when applicability is unresolved rather than guessing an exemption.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: jurisdiction-specific unit pricing, price accuracy, bottle deposit, SNAP/EBT, and retail food rules
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: No blocking ambiguity for the validated public baseline. The pack requires jurisdiction and tenant policy retrieval before applying local rules.

unresolved_ambiguity.handling: No blocking ambiguity for the validated public baseline. The lane refuses or escalates when documented authority is missing.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: FSMA traceability coverage and exemptions for specific retail establishments and food categories
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: No blocking ambiguity for the validated public baseline. The pack treats covered-food traceability evidence as required when applicability is unresolved rather than guessing an exemption.

research_to_spec_constraints: Inventory availability must be grounded in authoritative item, inventory, vendor, receiving, order, promotion, recall, and customer-impact records.
Traceability, recall, stop-sale, temperature, and food-safety uncertainty are stop-and-escalate conditions, not inventory optimization decisions.
Replenishment recommendations may name constraints and dependencies but must not approve spend, supplier changes, substitutions, or product release.
Customer-impact issues caused by shortages must route pricing, refund, fraud, safety, and policy exceptions to adjacent owners.
Tenant and jurisdiction rules are required before applying local pricing, refund, substitution, safety, SNAP/EBT, or approved-supplier exceptions.
